title: Strengthen indeterminate constraint test coverage for build and check
analysis:
This task strengthens CLI integration test coverage for indeterminate constraint scenarios. Three gaps exist:

1. **OK assertion gap (build only):** `check_indeterminate_constraint_exits_success` already asserts `stdout.contains("OK")` (line 128), but `build_indeterminate_constraint_exits_success` does not — it only checks for INDETERMINATE, Wrote, and absence of VIOLATED. Adding the OK assertion ensures the build path also verifies that the satisfied constraint (thickness > 2mm with thickness=5mm) reports correctly alongside the indeterminate one.

2. **Violated+indeterminate build test gap:** `cli_check.rs` already has `check_violated_and_indeterminate_exits_failure` (lines 171-200) using `bracket_violated_indeterminate.ri`, but `cli_build.rs` has no corresponding test. The build path exercises different code (geometry writing + constraint reporting). No new fixture needed — reuse existing `bracket_violated_indeterminate.ri`.

3. **All-indeterminate scenario gap:** No fixture or test covers the case where ALL constraints are indeterminate (no OK, no VIOLATED). The existing `bracket_indeterminate.ri` has one OK and one INDETERMINATE. A new `bracket_all_indeterminate.ri` fixture uses two auto params with constraints, plus concrete geometry params so build still produces output. Tests in both cli_build.rs and cli_check.rs assert exit 0, only INDETERMINATE in output, no OK.

All behavior already exists in the CLI (main.rs report_constraint_results + constraint_summary_message). This is purely test-coverage work — no implementation changes expected. Tests should pass immediately against the existing CLI.

prerequisites.description: Create fixture bracket_all_indeterminate.ri: structure with concrete geometry params (width=80mm, height=100mm, thickness=5mm) but only auto-param constraints (tolerance=auto → constraint tolerance > 0.1mm, clearance=auto → constraint clearance > 1mm). All constraints will be INDETERMINATE, none OK or VIOLATED.

steps.description: Add OK assertion to build_indeterminate_constraint_exits_success in cli_build.rs: assert!(stdout.contains("OK"), ...) to verify the satisfied thickness constraint is reported alongside the indeterminate one. Also add 'No constraint violations (some indeterminate)' positive assertion and '!contains All constraints satisfied' negative assertion to match the check test's thoroughness.

Add build_violated_and_indeterminate_exits_failure test to cli_build.rs using existing bracket_violated_indeterminate.ri fixture. Assert: exit code 1, stdout contains both VIOLATED and INDETERMINATE, stdout contains 'Some constraints violated.', geometry file still exists (build writes even on violation).

Add check_all_indeterminate_exits_success test to cli_check.rs using new bracket_all_indeterminate.ri fixture. Assert: exit 0, stdout contains INDETERMINATE, stdout does NOT contain OK, stdout does NOT contain VIOLATED, stdout contains 'No constraint violations (some indeterminate)', stdout does NOT contain 'All constraints satisfied'.

Add build_all_indeterminate_exits_success test to cli_build.rs using bracket_all_indeterminate.ri fixture. Assert: exit 0, stdout contains INDETERMINATE, stdout contains Wrote, stdout does NOT contain OK, stdout does NOT contain VIOLATED, stdout does NOT contain 'Some constraints violated', stdout contains 'No constraint violations (some indeterminate)', output_path exists.

design_decisions.decision: Reuse existing bracket_violated_indeterminate.ri instead of creating bracket_violated_with_indeterminate.ri
design_decisions.rationale: Task #692 (already done) created bracket_violated_indeterminate.ri with the exact semantics needed (thickness=1mm VIOLATED + tolerance=auto INDETERMINATE). Creating a second fixture with a slightly different name would be confusing duplication. The build test simply references the existing fixture.

design_decisions.decision: bracket_all_indeterminate.ri uses concrete geometry params with auto-only constraint params
design_decisions.rationale: Geometry params (width=80mm, height=100mm, thickness=5mm) must be concrete so box() can produce geometry for the build command. Only constraint-referenced params (tolerance, clearance) are auto, making ALL constraints indeterminate while still allowing successful geometry generation.

design_decisions.decision: No impl steps in the plan — all steps are test-only
design_decisions.rationale: The CLI already correctly handles all indeterminate constraint scenarios (report_constraint_results, constraint_summary_message in main.rs). This task is purely about strengthening test coverage. If any test unexpectedly fails, an impl step will be added reactively.

design_decisions.decision: Assert !contains(OK) in all-indeterminate tests to distinguish from the mixed indeterminate+OK scenario
design_decisions.rationale: The key differentiator between bracket_indeterminate.ri (has OK + INDETERMINATE) and bracket_all_indeterminate.ri (only INDETERMINATE) is the absence of OK in the output. This negative assertion is critical to verify the 'all indeterminate' scenario is distinct.

reuse.how: Each test file has its own fixture_path(name) that resolves to tests/fixtures/{name}. Reuse directly — no changes needed.
Created by task #692. Reuse for the build_violated_and_indeterminate test instead of creating a duplicate fixture with a different name.
Mirror this test's structure for the build variant, adding tempdir + output path + geometry-exists assertions per the build test pattern.
Use as template for build_all_indeterminate_exits_success, adjusting fixture name and swapping OK positive assertion for OK negative assertion.
constraint_summary_message() and report_constraint_results() already handle all scenarios correctly. Tests exercise these code paths without modification.
